Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.yaml

Class YamlParser

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.Parser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.parser.ReaderParser
org.apache.juneau.marshall.yaml.YamlParser
All Implemented Interfaces:
ArrayRecordReadable, RecordReadable
public class YamlParser extends ReaderParser implements RecordReadable, ArrayRecordReadable
Parses YAML text into a POJO model.
Media types

Handles Content-Type types: application/yaml, text/yaml

Description

This parser uses an indentation-aware state machine to parse YAML directly into POJOs without intermediate DOM objects. It supports block-style and flow-style mappings and sequences, plain and quoted scalars, comments, document markers, and standard YAML scalar types.

Limitations compared to JSON

The YAML parser has some limitations when compared to JsonParser:

  • Maps with non-String keys (Boolean, Date, Temporal, Enum) may not round-trip correctly when the target type is a generic Map with those key types. LinkedHashMap and TreeMap with String keys work reliably.
  • No strict vs non-strict mode; unlike JSON, there is no equivalent to JSON's lax parsing of comments, unquoted attributes, or concatenated strings.
  • YAML's indentation-based structure requires consistent formatting; malformed indentation can cause parsing errors where equivalent JSON would parse successfully.
Example input (Map of name/age):

name: Alice age: 30

Complex (nested object + array):

name: Alice age: 30 address: street: 123 Main St city: Boston state: MA tags: - a - b - c

Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
See Also:

      public YamlParser.Builder copy()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Creates a builder from this context object.

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      copy in class Parser
      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createSession

      public YamlParserSession.Builder createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class ReaderParser
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • getSession

      public YamlParserSession getSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Returns a session to use for this context.

      Note that subclasses may opt to return a reusable non-modifiable session.

      getSession in class ReaderParser
      Returns:
      A new session object.

    • parseRecords

      public RecordReader parseRecords(Object input) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the whole-value RecordReader using default session arguments. The real implementation lives on YamlParserSession.parseRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      parseRecords in interface RecordReadable
      Parameters:
      input - The input.
      Returns:
      A new RecordReader cursor.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred opening the underlying input.

    • parseArrayRecords

      public RecordReader parseArrayRecords(Object input) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator for the buffered array-element RecordReader using default session arguments. The real implementation lives on YamlParserSession.parseArrayRecords(Object).
      Specified by:
      parseArrayRecords in interface ArrayRecordReadable
      Parameters:
      input - The input.
      Returns:
      A buffered RecordReader.
      Throws:
      IOException - If a problem occurred reading the input.

    • isRecordStreaming

      public boolean isRecordStreaming()
      The YAML record cursor is buffered/RecordAdapter-backed, not O(1) streaming.
      Specified by:
      isRecordStreaming in interface RecordReadable
      Returns:
      Always false.

    • isArrayRecordStreaming

      public boolean isArrayRecordStreaming()
      The YAML array-record cursor is buffered/RecordAdapter-backed, not O(1) streaming.
